title: Identify and Prioritize User Views
breadcrumbs: Design Considerations for Performance and Scalability
text: Identify and Prioritize User Views A user view comprises the data needed for a business activity. The data can come from more than one modeled entity. Do not confuse the user view with the user interface of the application. It is not feasible to analyze all user views because it would be too time-consuming and probably not worth the effort. User views that are candidates for further examination have the following characteristics: Crucial to the business Have strict performance requirements Imply processing of high volumes Are needed frequently Comprise many entities (more than an average number) Are accessed by many components (more than an average number) Combine several application models For each user view that needs further examination, ensure that you have a good description of the user view, including the data needed and the objects accessed. It should provide enough information so that you can: Identify the need for access path analysis Identify access profiles. The access profile is the sequence of all access paths needed by a user view. Determine the moment of access of the access objects and estimate or calculate the access frequency Understand the impact on the implementation The information can be provided by a use case that describes the relevant business activity, including the interaction diagrams. Useful information can include: User view name (use case name) Purpose, indicating the functionality (use case activity) Actors interacting with the user view Type of interaction; for example, maintenance vs. data retrieval, performing calculations vs. producing a report, or a possible combination of these Importance from a business point of view Response time requirements Indication of the volumes processed Frequency of use Application models and entities accessed Security aspects Was this topic helpful?
